zh.Haccombe House, Newton Abbot, Devonshire: 英國戴文牛頓 阿博特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devon於1357年5月3日出生在英國戴文時，他的父親Sir Edward Courtenay27歲，母親Emeline Dauney27歲。他和Maud Camoys至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1419年12月5日在英國戴文迪維頓享年62歲。

zh-hans.Haccombe House, Newton Abbot, Devonshire: 英国戴文牛頓 阿博特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devon于1357年5月3日出生在英国戴文时，他的父亲Sir Edward Courtenay27岁，母亲Emeline Dauney27岁。他和Maud Camoys至少有4个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1419年12月5日在英国戴文迪維頓享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Courtenay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Courtney  .

variants.name: Courtney
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Courtenay near Sens in northern France, or some other place similarly named, from the name of a Gallo-Roman landlord, Curtenus (a derivative of Latin curtus ‘short’) + the locative suffix -acum.
Irish: English surname adopted for Ó Curnáin ‘descendant of Curnán’, an Old Irish personal name based on a diminutive of corn ‘horn’.

History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Athon de Courtenay, who lived in Courtenay in Loiret in the early 11th century. Renaud de Courtenay held land in Devon in the 12th century, and the family later held the title of Earls of Devon following the marriage of Hugh de Courtenay to Margaret de Bohun, granddaughter of King Edward I, in 1325.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devon was born on 3 May 1357, in Devon, England, his father, Sir Edward Courtenay, was 27 and his mother, Emeline Dauney, was 27. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Maud Camoys. He died on 5 December 1419, in Tiverton, Devon, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devon est né le 3 mai 1357, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir Edward Courtenay, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Emeline Dauney, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Maud Camoys. Il est décédé le 5 décembre 1419, à Tiverton, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devon am 3. Mai 1357 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Edward Courtenay 27 und seine Mutter Emeline Dauney 27. Er hatte mit Maud Camoys mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 5. Dezember 1419 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Tiverton, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devon è nato il il 3 maggio 1357 nel Devon, England, il suo padre, Sir Edward Courtenay, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Emeline Dauney, aveva 27 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Maud Camoys. Morì il 5 dicembre 1419, nel Tiverton, Devon, England, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devonが1357年5月3日にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父Sir Edward Courtenayは27歳，母Emeline Dauneyは27歳であった。彼はMaud Camoysとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1419年12月5日にイギリスデボンティバートンで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devon(이)가 1357년 5월 3일 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Edward Courtenay(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Emeline Dauney(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Maud Camoys(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1419년 12월 5일 영국 데폰 티버턴에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.3 May 1357–5 December 1419: 1357 оны Тавдугаар сарын 3–1419 оны Арванхоёрдугаар сарын 5

pt.3 May 1357–5 December 1419: 3 de maio de 1357–5 de dezembro de 1419
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devon nasceu em 3 de maio de 1357, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Edward Courtenay, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Emeline Dauney, tinha 27 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas com Maud Camoys. Ele faleceu em 5 de dezembro de 1419, em Tiverton, Devon, Inglaterra, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devon родился 3 мая 1357 г. в местности девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Edward Courtenay было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Emeline Dauney, 27. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Maud Camoys. Он умер 5 декабря 1419 г. в местности тивиртон, девон, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

es.3 May 1357–5 December 1419: 3 de mayo de 1357–5 de diciembre de 1419
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Courtenay 11rd Earl of Devon nació el 3 de mayo de 1357, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Edward Courtenay, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Emeline Dauney, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas con Maud Camoys. Murió el 5 de diciembre de 1419, en Tiverton, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
